title: Gateway to the Middle East and the Horn of Africa (5) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Introduces the development of Middle East and Horn of Africa cultures from ancient origins in Mesopotamia, Egypt, Anatolia, and Central Asia through the present day. Invites students into the world's earliest cultures, the diversity of its peoples, and their contributions to contemporary society.

title: Introduction to the Ancient Near East (5) SSc/A&H
catalogDescription: Surveys the peoples, places, and events of the ancient Near East. Examines the cultures of Mesopotamia, Egypt, Canaan, and Israel with an eye to each culture's cultural contributions. Pays special attention to shared cultural elements as well as distinguishing characteristics of the peoples of these regions.

title: Introduction to the Hebrew Bible: Old Testament (5) SSc/A&H
catalogDescription: Examines the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament) in translation and its relationship with literatures of ancient Near East. Comparisons drawn between Biblical text and literary works of Canaan, Egypt, Greece, Mesopotamia. Emphasis on the sophisticated literary techniques employed by Biblical writers. Offered: jointly with RELIG 240.

title: The Middle East in Song
catalogDescription: Surveys popular song as it has shaped modern culture and identity in the Middle East. Topics vary. All texts in English; no previous knowledge of other languages required. Credit/no-credit only.

title: Religions of the Ancient World
catalogDescription: A comparative exploration into ancient religious customs, rituals, and beliefs (ca. 3000-500 BCE). Focus on peoples of Mesopotamia, Egypt, Syria, and Israel. Topics include conceptions of worship and divinity, sacred space and time, and types and roles of priesthoods, divination, prayers, and afterlife beliefs. Recommended: NEAR E 201. Offered: A.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW recommended-preparation text: NEAR E 201.

title: The Biblical Prophets (3) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Explores the Biblical prophets (in translation) within their Near Eastern contexts. Historicity, literary and rhetorical sophistication, and ideological agendas. Seeks to uncover the meaning and distinctiveness of Israelite prophecy within the context of the larger Near East. No knowledge of the Bible required. Offered: jointly with RELIG 315.

title: The Archaeology of Biblical Israel
catalogDescription: Archaeology of ancient Israel (southern Levant). Covers the known archaeological material and Biblical and other contemporaneous textual sources to explore this topic, covering the archaeological cultures from the Middle Bronze Age to the end of the Babylonian Exile (2000-300 BCE). Offered: jointly with ARCHY 311.

title: Jewish Life in Literature and Film (5) SSc/A&H
catalogDescription: Major themes of Jewish life treated in modern narrative and cinema. Topics include religious tradition and modernity. Jewish immigration to America, responses to the Holocaust and Zionism. Course overlaps with: JEW ST 302/JSIS B 302. Offered: jointly with JEW ST 318.

title: Literature and the Holocaust
catalogDescription: Examines fiction, poetry, memoir, diaries, monuments, film, and pop culture from several languages and cultural milieus, with emphases on English and Hebrew. Topics include survivor testimony, shaping of collective memory, the second generation, Holocaust education and children's literature, gender and the Holocaust, and fantasy and humor as responses to catastrophe.

title: Jewish Poetry
catalogDescription: Examines elements of traditional Jewish prayers and worship with modern poems that draw on those classical sources. Examines poets from Europe, the Americas, the Near East and North Africa. Taught in English.

title: Bioethics: Secular and Jewish Perspectives
catalogDescription: Legal, ethical, scientific, and Jewish religious perspectives on contemporary medical and biomedical research practices. Legal and civil rights of women, people with disabilities, minors and minority or marginalized groups. Key differences between secular and Biblical/Rabbinic approaches in interpretation, analysis and application of bioethics, doctor-patient relationships; reproductive methods; abortion; euthanasia; and stem cell research. Offered: jointly with B H 339/JEW ST 339.

title: Prophecy in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam
catalogDescription: Looks at the phenomenon of prophecy in Jewish, Christian, and Muslim thought and writing from antiquity to modernity. Traces the development of prophetic expression in the Hebrew Bible, the New Testament, and the Quran. Surveys major themes and covers various eras, including prophecy in the American context.

title: Jews and Arabs (5) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: An introduction to the Jewish-Muslim encounter: a look at exchange, symbiosis, liminality, and confrontation between these two kindred religio-cultural systems, from the rise of Islam, to the end of its Classical Age - six centuries wherein the majority of the world's Jews lived among Muslim majorities.

title: Seminar in Middle Eastern Languages and Cultures
catalogDescription: Covers issues of methodology as well as linguistic, philosophical, literary critical, and rhetorical topics. Focuses on developing academic presentation and communication skills. Includes supervised readings and group discussion. Credit/no-credit only.
